campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss and vote on changes to business license tax rates in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the annual update to these tax rates, which required a formal amendment to city rules. The council deliberated publicly on the proposal before making a final decision.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates being increased or adjusted
Annual finance department review and update
Changes to Campbell Municipal Code rules
Public council deliberation and voting on the tax changes

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After listening to public input, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipping the first formal reading step to speed up the process. They plan to vote on and adopt this ordinance at their next regular meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for resident input on a proposed ordinance
First reading of ordinance was waived to expedite adoption
Ordinance scheduled for final vote and adoption at next council meeting
Process allows public comment before city law takes effect

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the city's finances from February, and authorize the Town Manager to sign contracts for construction work and professional services. The council also moved forward with adopting a resolution related to town improvements.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes (both public and closed sessions)
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other construction services
Approved multi-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve routine meeting minutes and make decisions on several projects affecting the city. Key items included moving forward with replacing an elevator at the Terminal, setting up a process to hire companies for cleaning up homeless encampments, updating a digital billboard advertising agreement with Clear Channel, and approving funding for a firefighter memorial event in District 7.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project moving forward
New process to hire encampment cleanup service providers
Digital billboard advertising agreement amendment with Clear Channel
City funding approved for 'Light the Night' firefighter memorial event

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve previous meeting notes and hear from residents about issues they care about. The main focus was reviewing requests from neighborhood groups asking for city funding and deciding how much money each group should receive for the upcoming budget year.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 budget
Attendance tracking at neighborhood meetings
Committee updates and staff reports

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special study session on April 28, 2026 to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate both in person and online to watch the proceedings and submit public comments.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period for residents
In-person and online participation options

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council met to discuss buying or selling a piece of property on Finch Avenue with the local school district, with the city manager and attorney leading those negotiations. The council also reviewed a program called Silicon Valley Hopper and discussed plans to get grant money for it in the future.
keyTopics: Negotiating to buy or sell property at 10480 Finch Avenue with Cupertino Union School District
Details about price and payment terms for the Finch Avenue property
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be put on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and to approve a new labor contract with the city's firefighters. One item that had been scheduled was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs approved for FIFA World Cup 2026 events at Levi's Stadium
New firefighter union contract negotiated for 2026-2029
Ordinance allowing supergraphic signage on local buildings

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss and approve the city's spending plans for the next several years. They reviewed budgets for 2027-2031, talked about how to spend money on parks and trails in the Innovation District, and made updates to how the council conducts its meetings.
keyTopics: Approval of 5-year capital spending plan (2027-2031)
FY 2026-27 operating budget presentation and feedback
Innovation District parks and trails master plan adoption
Updates to City Council meeting procedures and rules

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a meeting on April 29, 2026, where they considered a proposal to divide a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into nine separate residential lots so that nine new single-family homes and seven smaller accessory units (junior ADUs) can be built there. The council also handled routine business like electing new leadership and setting the meeting schedule.
keyTopics: Subdivision proposal for 4103 Old Trace Road creating 9 new single-family homes
Seven junior accessory dwelling units (smaller rental units) planned for the site
Project exempt from standard environmental review under state law
Election of Council Chair and Vice Chair
Director's report and meeting schedule updates
